title: Avoid using raw types in Spring
description: Using generic types raw (without type arguments) prevents compile-time type checking for expressions that use these type parameters. Explicit type casts are necessary for them, which do perform a runtime type check that may fail with a ClassCastException.
recommendation: Add the type parameters to fix this issue.
impact: The rule does not raise an issue for the simple instanceof operator, which checks against runtime types where type parameter information has been erased. Since it does not return a rawly typed instance but a boolean value, it does not prevent compile-time type checking.

checks.message: Add the type parameters to fix this issue.
